seoTitle: How to Integrate ClickUp with GitHub and Sync Commits
metaDescription: Learn how to connect GitHub repositories to ClickUp, link commits to task IDs, and automate status changes when pull requests open.
intro: Engineering teams often want their task tracker to reflect what's actually happening in the codebase without manual status updates. This guide covers connecting a GitHub repository to ClickUp, referencing task IDs in your commit messages and branch names, and setting up an automation rule that shifts a task's status the moment a related pull request opens.

steps.heading: Open the App Center
steps.body: In ClickUp, click your profile avatar and launch the App Center to find available integrations.

steps.heading: Connect GitHub
steps.body: Scroll to the Development section, find GitHub, and click Connect to start the authorization flow.

steps.heading: Authorize your GitHub account
steps.body: Pass the GitHub OAuth consent screen and select the account or organization you want to link.

steps.heading: Map repositories to Spaces
steps.body: Back in ClickUp, link specific GitHub repositories to individual Spaces to keep code tracking organized and isolated.

steps.heading: Reference task IDs in your commits
steps.body: Copy a task's unique ID from the task card and include it in your branch name or commit message to link development activity back to that task.

steps.heading: Set up automated status changes
steps.body: Build an automation rule so a task automatically shifts from 'In Progress' to 'In Review' the moment a linked pull request is opened.

faq.question: Do I need to reference the task ID in every commit?
faq.answer: Referencing the task ID in your branch name or commit message is what links your GitHub activity to a specific ClickUp task, so it's needed for the automatic tracking to work.

faq.question: Can I connect multiple repositories to one ClickUp Space?
faq.answer: Yes, you can map several repositories to a single Space depending on how your team's projects are organized.

faq.question: Will merging a pull request automatically close the task?
faq.answer: That depends on how you configure your automation rules, since you can set different triggers for opening, reviewing, and merging pull requests.

faq.question: Is the GitHub integration available on ClickUp's free plan?
faq.answer: Development integrations like GitHub are generally available across ClickUp plans, though some automation limits may apply on the free tier.
